regions.us-ports: Today, all US East Coast ports including Charleston, Savannah, Jacksonville, and Miami are seeing generally favorable conditions with light winds from the south-southwest at 10-12 knots and mostly clear skies; rain chances remain low today, below 5%, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing drizzle and elevated rain probabilities around 34% in Charleston and similar conditions likely spreading to nearby ports such as Savannah and Jacksonville, as winds shift to the north at around 13 knots with drizzle increasing to a 42% chance by day 3, which could cause some delays or adjustments to outdoor activities. On the West Coast, ports including Long Beach, Los Angeles, and San Francisco will maintain clear skies and light southwest winds near 6 knots today with no significant rain expected through the next two days. Beyond 48 hours, the trend toward increased drizzle and a rising rain probability to 42% in Charleston and adjacent southeastern ports suggests cruise operations should prepare for intermittent wet conditions and possibly reduced visibility during docking and tendering. All vessels and crew should monitor updated wind shifts and drizzle forecasts closely, especially in the southeastern US ports, to ensure passenger safety and timely port operations.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, conditions at Nassau and nearby ports such as Freeport and Bimini feature light east-northeast winds at 5 knots with clear skies and only a 1% chance of rain, allowing smooth embarkation and shore excursions with minimal weather impacts. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect easterly winds increasing to 8-10 knots accompanied by light drizzle and rain probabilities rising to 20-22 percent, which may affect outdoor activities and tender operations at Grand Cayman, Key West, and the northern Dominican Republic ports including Puerto Plata and Santo Domingo. On April 26, winds shift northeast at 7 knots with a return to clear skies and a low 7 percent rain chance, improving conditions for Falmouth, La Romana, and Samaná for both port operations and day excursions. Passengers and crew should monitor these light but persistent drizzle periods through tomorrow as they may cause slick surfaces on piers and excursion trails, but no marine wind warnings are expected. Beyond 48 hours, the trend toward lighter winds and clearer skies should support stable cruising and port calls throughout the Central Caribbean and Bahamas region.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, winds are light from the south-southeast at 3 knots with overcast skies and no rain expected, supporting normal port operations and shore excursions across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect an elevated rain chance of 40 percent with north-northeast winds increasing to 7 knots and persistent overcast conditions, which may impact outdoor activities and embarkation processes especially in San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix. By day three, rain showers become likely with a 53 percent chance and north-northeast winds near 9 knots, potentially causing delays or adjustments to shore excursions in St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Passengers and crew should prepare for reduced visibility and wet conditions during port calls beyond 48 hours, as increasing moisture and steady winds could lead to localized disruptions in all Eastern Caribbean ports. We will continue to monitor these trends closely to provide timely updates for safe and efficient cruise operations.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, Cozumel is experiencing southeast winds at 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero percent chance of rain, so port operations and shore excursions there will proceed smoothly with minimal weather impacts. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift slightly more southerly at 8 knots with partly cloudy to overcast skies and rain chances ranging from 15% to 3%, which may affect Costa Maya and Belize City by causing brief interruptions to outdoor activities. Roatan should expect southeast winds steady near 8 to 10 knots with mostly partly cloudy conditions and minimal rain probability, keeping marine operations steady. Beyond 48 hours, the southeast wind is expected to increase to 10 knots with clear skies and no rain chance, signaling stable conditions for continued cruise schedules and port calls throughout the Western Caribbean. Passengers and crew should remain aware of the gradual wind increase but can anticipate consistent and manageable weather conditions for the coming days.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today in the Southern Caribbean, expect steady east winds at 11 knots with overcast skies and a negligible 0% chance of rain in Aruba, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions with minimal weather impact. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will hold steady from the east at 13 knots with continued overcast conditions and a slight increase in rain probability to 5% in Aruba, while Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena should prepare for similar conditions and isolated light showers. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds will ease slightly to 10 knots, but overcast skies will persist with a higher rain chance of 33% around Aruba, potentially causing minor delays or adjustments in outdoor activities at all four ports. All vessels and excursion teams should monitor these developing trends closely to adjust scheduling and ensure passenger safety during embarkation and disembarkation.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today moderate easterly winds at 12 knots will bring partly cloudy skies with a 35% chance of rain showers across Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, and Grenada, so expect intermittent wet conditions that may impact shore excursions and embarkation timing. Ports such as Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, and St. Vincent will see similar easterly winds near 12 knots with a 35% rain probability, requiring readiness for localized light showers during boarding operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift slightly to east-southeast at 13 knots with continued 35% rain chances, particularly affecting Barbados, St. Lucia, and Martinique, where overcast skies may reduce visibility for tenders and outdoor activities. Beyond 48 hours, the wind is forecast to increase to 15 knots from the east-southeast with overcast conditions and a reduced 13% rain chance, indicating improving conditions but persistent cloud cover for all Lesser Antilles ports. Crew should monitor vessel maneuvering in moderate easterly winds while passengers should plan for occasional showers impacting shore plans today and tomorrow.
regions.los-angeles: Today at the Los Angeles, San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island ports, winds are light from the WSW at 5 knots with overcast skies and zero percent rain chance, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions without weather delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect increasing westerly winds up to 13 knots and a significant rise in rain probability to 57 percent with persistent overcast conditions impacting port operations especially at Los Angeles and Long Beach where drizzle could reduce visibility and slow disembarkation. By day three, sustained west winds will strengthen to 16 knots accompanied by a 63 percent chance of drizzle, notably at Catalina Island and Marina del Rey, necessitating caution for tender operations and outdoor excursions. Passengers and crew should anticipate damp conditions and potential minor delays beyond 48 hours as the steady drizzle and overcast skies persist with increasing winds, though no marine wind warnings are currently in effect. Stay alert for updated advisories as these evolving conditions demand close monitoring to maintain safe and efficient cruise operations.
regions.ensenada: Today, Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos are experiencing light winds from the south-southwest at 1 knot with clear skies and a 0% chance of rain, supporting smooth port operations and uninterrupted shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect westerly winds increasing to 10 knots with overcast skies and a 56% probability of rain impacting Ensenada and Punta Banda, which may cause delays or modifications to outdoor activities and tender operations. By day three, winds will strengthen to 13 knots from the west with an 88% chance of drizzle across all three ports, including Islas Todos Santos, likely reducing visibility and requiring extra caution during embarkation and disembarkation. Passengers and crew should anticipate increasing moisture and cloud cover through midweek, with elevated rain chances possibly affecting scheduling and deck safety protocols beyond 48 hours. We recommend staying updated on announcements as these developing conditions could impact vessel maneuvering and shore excursion availability.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today in the Cabo San Lucas region, we have NNW winds sustained at 14 knots with clear skies and zero percent chance of rain, providing excellent conditions for safe port operations and uninterrupted shore excursions at Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect sustained NW winds increasing slightly to 16 knots with partly cloudy skies on day one and overcast conditions on day two, maintaining zero percent rain probability; these conditions will similarly affect all three ports, so plan accordingly for potential reduced sunshine during outdoor excursions. Beyond 48 hours, from day three onward, NW winds will hold steady at 16 knots with overcast skies continuing and no rain expected, which supports ongoing smooth embarkation and disembarkation activities across the region. Mariners should note that wind speeds remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, ensuring no significant impact on vessel maneuvering or passenger comfort. Overall, the forecast supports continued safe and efficient cruise operations around Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz through April 26.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the northwest at 2 knots with partly cloudy skies and zero chance of rain, providing smooth conditions for embarkation and disembarkation operations at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata ports. Passengers and crew should remain aware that while skies will be mostly cloudy, stable light winds and zero rain chances will support safe and efficient cruise operations throughout the Western Mediterranean.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today in the Central Mediterranean, Naples is experiencing light northerly winds at 3 knots with clear skies and zero chance of rain, creating ideal conditions for smooth embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions in Naples, Civitavecchia, and nearby ports like Livorno and La Spezia. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to NNW winds increasing to 9 knots with overcast skies but no rain, which may slightly reduce visibility in Genoa and Nice but should not disrupt port operations or scheduled excursions. By day three, winds will settle from the southwest at 6 to 7 knots with partly cloudy skies and no rain, improving conditions for sailing and shore activities across Sardinia, Corsica, Monaco, and Marseille. There are no marine wind warnings or precipitation threats forecast through this period, so cruise operations from Venice to Dubrovnik should proceed without weather-related delays. Passengers and crew should monitor for minor changes in cloud cover but anticipate stable and dry conditions throughout the Central Mediterranean corridor.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, winds are light from the WSW at 1 knot with clear skies and zero rain chance in Athens (Piraeus), ensuring smooth conditions for embarkation, disembarkation, and shore excursions across the Eastern Mediterranean ports including Santorini, Mykonos, and Rhodes.
